children.heading: Research and pilot project program
children.label: a Research and pilot project program
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, during the period beginning , and ending , the Secretary shall develop and implement a research project program for the development of supplemental and alternative crops (including canola), using such funds as are appropriated to the Secretary each fiscal year under this chapter.October 1, 1986September 30, 2023

children.heading: Importance to producers
children.label: b Importance to producers
children.text: The development of supplemental and alternative crops for agronomic rotational purposes and as a habitat for honey bees and other pollinators is of critical importance to producers of agricultural commodities.

children.heading: Research funding, special or competitive grants, etc.; program requirements; agreements, grants and other arrangements
children.label: c Research funding, special or competitive grants, etc.; program requirements; agreements, grants and other arrangements

children.text: The Secretary shall make competitive grants to further the purposes of this section in the implementation of a comprehensive and integrated program.

children.label: A
children.text: an examination of the adaptation of supplemental and alternative crops;

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment and extension of various methods of planting, cultivating, harvesting, and processing supplemental and alternative crops;

children.label: C
children.text: the transfer of such applied research to on-farm practice as soon as practicable;

children.label: D
children.text: the establishment through grants, cooperative agreements, or other means of such processing, storage, and transportation facilities for supplemental and alternative crops as the Secretary determines will facilitate the achievement of a successful program; and

children.label: E
children.text: the application of such other resources and expertise as the Secretary considers appropriate to support the program.

children.label: A
children.text: to conduct comprehensive resource and infrastructure assessments;

children.label: B
children.text: to develop and introduce supplemental and alternative income-producing crops;

children.label: C
children.text: to develop and expand domestic and export markets for such crops;

children.label: D
children.text: to provide technical assistance to farm owners and operators, marketing cooperatives, and others;

children.label: E
children.text: to conduct fundamental and applied research related to the development of new commercial products derived from natural plant material (including hemp (as defined in section 1639 of this title)) for industrial, medical, and agricultural applications; ando

children.label: F
children.text: to participate with colleges and universities, other Federal agencies, and private sector entities in conducting research described in subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Use of expertise and resources of other Federal agencies and land-grant colleges and universities
children.label: d Use of expertise and resources of other Federal agencies and land-grant colleges and universities
children.text: The Secretary shall use the expertise and resources of the Agricultural Research Service, the National Institute of Food and Agriculture, and the land-grant colleges and universities for the purpose of carrying out this section.
such sums as are necessary for fiscal year 2013;
$1,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2018; and
$2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.
